mnemonics.title: Cloud Service Models
mnemonics.content: IaaS: Infrastructure - Virtual Machines | PaaS: Platform - Development Environment | SaaS: Software - Complete Applications. Think: I-P-S as in 'I'm Providing Software'

mnemonics.title: Deployment Models
mnemonics.content: Public: Everyone can use | Private: Single org | Hybrid: Mix of both | Community: Shared by group. Remember: PPHC = Public Private Hybrid Community

mnemonics.title: Green Cloud Benefits
mnemonics.content: SAVE: Sustainable, Automated, Virtualized, Efficient. Or remember: 'Cloud is Green because it reduces hardware scene'

acronyms.usage: Provides virtualized computing resources like servers and storage over the Internet
Provides platform for developers to build applications without managing infrastructure
Delivers software applications over the Internet on subscription basis
Metric to measure data center energy efficiency (lower is better)
Complete cost including hardware, software, maintenance, and energy
Primary processor in servers handling computational tasks
Greenhouse gas that cloud computing aims to reduce through efficiency

visualTips.visualization: Draw a pyramid: Bottom = IaaS (basic building blocks), Middle = PaaS (platform layer), Top = SaaS (complete applications). Each level includes the one below it.
Show two buildings: Traditional = mostly empty rooms (5-15% used), Cloud = nearly full building (60-80% used). This shows efficiency through shared resources.
Draw a pie chart: If PUE=1.2, then 1 part cooling/overhead, 5 parts IT (1/1.2 = 83% efficient). Lower PUE = bigger IT slice = more efficient.
Draw apartment building: Each tenant has private space but shares common infrastructure (water, electricity, elevator). In cloud, each customer has isolated data but shares hardware.

analogies.analogy: Like utility companies (electricity, water): You pay for what you use, don't build your own power plant. Cloud provides computing 'on demand'.
Like a magic trick: One table (physical server) appears to have many tables (virtual machines) underneath it, but it's really just one being shown multiple times.
Like a rubber band: Stretches (scales up) when you pull harder (more demand), shrinks (scales down) when you release (less demand). Automatically adapts.
Like an office building: Multiple companies share the same building, each with their own locked office, but share the lobby, elevator, and utilities.
Like carpooling: Instead of everyone driving their own car (individual servers), people share rides (cloud resources), reducing traffic (energy) and pollution (emissions).
